analysis.consistency: Consistent with standard D&D 5e feature presentation for high-level class features.
analysis.quality: High quality. The feature is clearly defined, setting specific level requirements, spell level choices, casting mechanics (at will vs. slot usage), and the process for changing the selected spells.

mechanics.effect: Allows at-will casting (without expending a spell slot) of one chosen 1st-level and one chosen 2nd-level wizard spell from the spellbook, cast at their lowest level. Higher level casting still requires a slot.
limitations: Casting at higher levels still costs slots.
Must be spells currently in the wizard's spellbook.
mechanics.maintenance: Spells can be swapped after an 8-hour study period.

analysis.notes: This feature provides a significant utility boost at very high levels, representing ultimate command over a couple of foundational spells. The casting limitation (lowest level only) prevents it from being overpowered while still granting excellent resource management.
